_meta.description: The ONLY source of publicly published Provenance Delta numbers. RESULTS.md is generated from this file (tools/build_results_page.py). A figure may appear under 'validated' ONLY if it cleared the no-overclaim gate; everything else is 'illustrative' and labelled as such.
_meta.gate: validated = >=2 independent judges in consensus (judge != subject) + inter-judge agreement reported + >=3 runs + confidence intervals.
_meta.hiddenEvalNote: Hidden-eval prompts are never published; only aggregates + hashed commitments.

externalEvals.harness: agent/external_eval.py — exact-match vs external gold (no LLM judge)
externalEvals.note: Objective exact-match against external gold. This reports the BASE MODEL's accuracy via Sophia's external-eval harness and validates the harness end-to-end; it is NOT a claim about Sophia's provenance gate or any Sophia-specific capability. N=100 of the 1319-item test split.

verifierEvals.scoring: objective exact-match vs ground-truth expectPass; deterministic verifier, no LLM judge.
verifierEvals.note: Measures the VERIFIER's accuracy at catching fabricated legal citations (the Mata v. Avianca failure mode), not any model's. Honest bounds: tiny, constructed benchmark (N=8) and the result is capped by the bundled register's completeness, so this validates the extraction + fail-closed gate logic end-to-end — it is NOT a headline capability claim. Reproduce: python tools/run_legal_citation_bench.py.

illustrative.modelNote: uncensored 8B local tune (the population the gate is for)
illustrative.caveat: Single judge. An independent Claude audit panel re-judged all 46 cases and found this judge OVER-counted (76% agreement, 10 false positives), so the validated alone-rate is ~21.7%, not 42.8%. This row is illustrative ONLY and must not be quoted as a headline.

audit.note: Independent Claude panel vs the DeepSeek judge on 46 false cases: 76% agreement; validated alone-rate 21.7% (10/46) vs 41.3% single-judge. Judge choice dominates the absolute number.
robustRegardlessOfJudge: 0% false-positive cost (the gate never broke a correct answer)
the delta is positive and real
the effect tracks a model's propensity-to-assert, not its size
